original_abstract: Despite the foundational role of faith, many contemporary Christian families struggle with unhappiness due to a lack of understanding regarding biblical family management. This study explores Psalm 127 as a central framework for establishing a joyful household aligned with divine will, positing that family life devoid of spiritual involvement inevitably lacks true fulfillment. Utilizing a qualitative descriptive methodology, the research examines the theological implications of God’s sovereignty over the domestic sphere. The findings suggest that endeavors independent of God result in futility, whereas acknowledging His ultimate authority invites divine intervention. Ultimately, a family that center’s its life on God’s leadership experiences genuine happiness, as He actively works within the home to cultivate lasting joy and peace.
humanized_abstract: While faith is seen as the foundation of a successful home, many families in the western world are unhappy without realising that they do not understand biblical principles of family management. This study has taken Psalm 127 as its focal point in order to explore the idea of creating a happy home, submitted to the Lord's will and exploring the theological implications of God's sovereignty in relation to the home, concluding that only futility can result from human efforts unaided by God and that only the home that has God as its focal point will be a happy home submitting to God, resulting in Him actively working in our homes to give us the joy and peace we seek.
